phrases.intent_en: Ask where to get the walking route map
phrases.text_local: Buenos días, ¿dónde consigo un mapa de la ruta a pie?
phrases.pronunciation: BWEH-nohs DEE-ahs, DOHN-deh kon-SEE-goh oon MAH-pah deh lah ROO-tah ah pyeh?

responses.english: At the ticket office, available for free.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where to access the virtual tour
phrases.text_local: Buenos días, ¿dónde accedo al tour virtual?
phrases.pronunciation: BWEH-nohs DEE-ahs, ¿DOHN-deh ak-SEH-doh al toor beer-TOO-ahl?

responses.english: At the visitor center, ground floor.
responses.phonetic: EHN EHL THEHNT-roh DEH bee-see-TAHN-tehs PLAHN-tah BAH-hah
responses.local: En el centro de visitantes, planta baja.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the best preserved wall section is
phrases.text_local: ¿Dónde está el tramo de muralla mejor conservado?
phrases.pronunciation: ¿DOHN-deh eh-STAH el TRAH-moh deh moo-RAH-yah meh-HOHR kon-ser-VAH-doh?

responses.english: Follow signs towards the Angel Gate.
responses.phonetic: SEE-gah LAHS seh-NYAH-lehs ah-THEE-ah LAH poo-EHR-tah DEHL ÁNHEHL
responses.local: Siga las señales hacia la Puerta del Ángel.

phrases.intent_en: Ask where the best-preserved section of Roman wall is
phrases.text_local: Disculpe, ¿dónde está el tramo mejor conservado de la muralla romana?
phrases.pronunciation: dees-KOOL-peh, DOHN-deh es-TAH ehl TRAH-moh meh-HOR kon-ser-BAH-doh deh lah moo-RAH-yah roh-MAH-nah
phrases.cultural_note: Zaragoza's Roman walls date from the 1st century BC when the city was called Caesaraugusta — the name Zaragoza derives from it.

responses.english: In the Pablo Gargallo Park, next to the Central Market.
responses.phonetic: ehn ehl PAR-keh PAH-bloh gar-GAH-yoh, HOON-toh al mehr-KAH-doh sehn-TRAL.
responses.local: En el Parque Pablo Gargallo, junto al Mercado Central.

phrases.intent_en: Ask if entry to the Roman wall site is free
phrases.text_local: ¿La visita a las murallas es gratuita?
phrases.pronunciation: lah bee-SEE-tah ah lahs moo-RAH-yahs ehs gra-TWEE-tah

responses.phonetic: see, ehl ak-SEH-soh eks-teh-RYOR ehs LEE-breh.
responses.local: Sí, el acceso exterior es libre.
